channel.title: compose on ilo
channel.description: Recent content in compose on ilo

item.title: Customize Environment
item.description: The ilo compose command looks for a docker-compose.yml file by default and uses the dev service to open your new shell. Specify any option, additional volumes, custom images and commands in that file in order to customize your build environment according to your needs. Depending on your preferred runtime you probably have to change the location of the file to use with the --file flag, e.g. pods-compose will look for a pods-compose.

item.title: Options
item.description: The ilo compose command can be configured with the following command line options. From your terminal, use ilo compose --help to get a list of all options, and their default values. --build The --build option can be used to build images before opening a new shell. Use this option in case you rely on local Dockerfiles. # build images before opening shell $ ilo compose --build # do not build images before opening shell $ ilo compose --build=false # do not build images before opening shell $ ilo compose By default, --build is not enabled.

item.title: Runtimes
item.description: ilo compose by default searches your local system for supported runtimes. It prefers docker-compose over podman-compose over pods-compose over footloose over vagrant at the moment. In order to force the usage of a specific runtime, use the --runtime flag. docker-compose Force ilo to use docker-compose like this: $ ilo shell --runtime docker-compose # alias $ ilo shell --runtime dc podman-compose Force ilo to use podman-compose like this: $ ilo shell --runtime podman-copose # alias $ ilo shell --runtime pc
